steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "there is no better example of the form" establishes that Mildred Jean Walter's work is unsurpassed — it's the supreme standard.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "surpassed or gone beyond" — "no one has ever ______ Mildred Jean Walter's work" must convey that no one has topped it. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — convened: gathered or assembled — convening Walter's work makes no sense in this context of surpassing quality. ✗
  • Choice B — cited: referenced or quoted — being cited is positive, but "no one has ever cited" her work would mean she's ignored, contradicting the praise. ✗
  • Choice C — eclipsed: surpassed or overshadowed — "no one has eclipsed" her work means no author has produced a better example, matching "there is no better example of the form." ✓
  • Choice D — augmented: added to or increased — augmenting her work means expanding it, not surpassing it as a standard. ✗
That is the correct answer.
